meta.title: 6-Layer Architecture Pattern
meta.excerpt: The foundational N-tier data access pattern that separates DTOs, Mappers, Repositories, and View Models into clean, testable layers.

seo.metaDescription: Learn the N-tier data layer pattern that separates concerns across DTOs, Mappers, Repositories, and View Models for clean, maintainable code.
seo.keywords: 6-layer architecture, N-tier pattern, repository pattern, data access layer, separation of concerns

blocks.content: The N-Tier Pattern Overview

The N-Tier pattern is a time-proven architecture from enterprise systems that cleanly separates data access into distinct, testable layers. Each layer has a single responsibility and communicates with adjacent layers through well-defined contracts.
Layer 1: DTOs (Data Transfer Objects)

The DTO layer represents the exact shape of data as it comes from your external source (Strapi API, REST API, GraphQL, database, etc.). DTOs contain no business logic and are validated with Zod at import time.
Characteristics of good DTOs: exact match to API response shape, no transformations, validation at module init time ensures bad data is caught immediately.
Layer 2: Mappers
Mappers transform DTOs into Domain Models. They flatten nested structures, apply defaults, compute new values, and create a canonical domain representation.
Layer 3: Repository (Data Access Layer)

The Repository provides a clean interface for data access. It's server-only (using Next.js 'use server' directive) and abstracts away the data source, enabling easy swaps between Strapi, REST APIs, databases, etc.
Layer 4: View Models
View Models transform Domain Models into UI-specific shapes. They include computed properties for display, formatting, and additional data fetches (like related articles).
Layer 5: Pages (Server Components)
Pages are React Server Components that orchestrate data access: they call repository methods, transform to view models, and pass props to UI components.
Why This Pattern?
This architecture provides several critical benefits:

items.title: Clear Responsibility
items.description: Each layer has a single job: DTOs match API, Mappers transform, Repository provides access, ViewModels format, Pages orchestrate

items.title: Easy Testing
items.description: Each layer can be tested independently with simple unit tests or integration tests at the Repository level

items.title: Backend Agnostic
items.description: Switch data sources by swapping Repository implementation; rest of codebase is unaffected

items.title: Type Safety
items.description: TypeScript inference flows through each layer; breaking changes are caught at compile time

items.title: Reusability
items.description: Same Repository can serve multiple pages; same ViewModels across SSG, SSR, and dynamic routes

blocks.title: This Is More Than Just Code Organization
blocks.content: This pattern is proven in enterprise systems and C#/.NET for decades. It works because it separates concerns precisely: data shape concerns, transformation concerns, access concerns, display concerns. When a requirement changes, you know exactly which layer to modify.
